title: Resident Evil 5
subtitle: (the video game)
review: If this game wasn't co-op, with good netcode and low system requirements, I would be rating it much lower. As long as you can jump into it with a friend and interact with each other, and experience the ropey story and repetitive gameplay together, then you can keep each other entertained through the long levels full of combat gauntlets. Any kind of action that wasn't just shooting waves of zombie worm-men, was relegated to the non-interactive cinematics, which used such a shaky and quick-cutting camera that they were nearly impossible to follow. The characters never developed throughout the game, and even the interesting yet problematic African setting fizzled out two thirds of the way through, as you end up in the usual evil corporation underground laboratories again. The satisfaction of popping heads, and the dynamic of having to cover your partner and divide the task of carrying equipment and distracting enemies, were the only elements that were consistently fun through to the end.
spoilers: I did not read the incredibly lengthy expository text dumps accessible through the main menu, but if they were written in the same style as the notes you find in the game world, then I know I didn't miss anything of any quality. The plot makes a lot of references to a more exciting sounding mission that happened beforehand, and that is represented by a DLC package that is unlocked after beating the main campaign.
